articles.title: India's FY27 growth likely to slow down to 6.6% on energy stress, sub-par monsoon: S&P
articles.summary: India's economic growth is projected to slow to 6.6% in the current fiscal year, according to S P Global Ratings. This slowdown is attributed to energy market pressures, a potentially weak monsoon, and a global economic deceleration. Higher energy and fertilizer costs are expected to fuel inflation, potentially leading to a policy rate hike in the latter half of the fiscal year.

articles.title: Pocket FM winds down microdrama to focus on audio dramas, overseas market
articles.summary: Audio entertainment firm Pocket FM is shutting down its microdrama vertical, Pocket TV, to concentrate on its core audio dramas and international expansion. This strategic shift precedes a potential public listing, as the company prioritizes profitability. While short video dramas are booming in India, Pocket FM is returning to its strengths in audio, where it has established content and monetization.

articles.title: India eyes chip investments at Pax Silica Summit
articles.summary: India is actively seeking semiconductor investments and multilateral funding at the Pax Silica summit in Washington D.C. The nation aims to leverage the US's $250 million seed fund to bolster its growing semiconductor manufacturing and design capabilities. This initiative is part of a broader global effort to diversify supply chains and reduce reliance on China, with key European nations also joining the coalition.

articles.title: China's 360 says it has developed tools to match Anthropic's Mythos
articles.summary: China's 360 Security Technology has developed tools to match Anthropic's Mythos, a direct competitor to American AI vulnerability detection systems. The company's founder highlighted the critical need for such technologies in national security. The tools aim to identify software flaws to mitigate cyber threats and secure a tactical advantage.
articles.raw_summary: China's 360 Security Technology has launched 'Yitian Tulong,' a direct competitor to American AI vulnerability detection systems like Anthropic's Mythos. Founder Zhou Hongyi highlighted the critical need for such technologies in national security, bringing attention to issues surrounding 'one-way transparency.' The firm asserts that its 'Tulongfeng' tool effectively identifies software flaws to mitigate cyber threats and secure a tactical advantage.

articles.title: OpenAI unveils custom chip it designed with Broadcom to boost its AI infrastructure
articles.summary: OpenAI has developed a custom AI chip called Jalapeno in collaboration with Broadcom. This chip is designed to boost OpenAI's AI infrastructure and reduce its reliance on Nvidia's GPUs. The development of this chip is a significant step in OpenAI's multi-generation chip development strategy.
articles.raw_summary: OpenAI has unveiled its first custom AI chip, Jalapeno, developed with Broadcom to boost its infrastructure. This move aims to overcome computing power shortages and reduce reliance on Nvidia's GPUs. Designed for AI inference, the chip is comparable to Nvidia's and Google's offerings. OpenAI plans to deploy Jalapeno by year-end, marking a significant step in its multi-generation chip development strategy.

articles.title: India-China border trade via Lipulekh Pass to resume after 6 yrs
articles.summary: Border trade between India and Tibet is set to resume through the Lipulekh pass on June 26, after a six-year halt due to the pandemic. The first batch of 26 Indian traders, including helpers, will enter Chinese territory. A customs office is operational, and preparations are underway for a second batch. Traders anticipate logistical hurdles and will assess goods stored for years before seeking government aid for potential losses.

The first batch of 26 Indian traders, including helpers, will enter Chinese territory. A customs office is operational, and preparations are underway for a second batch. Traders anticipate logistical hurdles and will assess goods stored for years before seeking government aid for potential losses.

articles.title: WTO to set up dispute panel in China's case against India's solar, IT goods measures
articles.summary: China has successfully established a World Trade Organization panel to challenge India's solar cell, module, and IT goods policies. Beijing alleges these measures, including tariffs and incentives, unfairly target Chinese products. India had previously blocked the request, but the WTO body accepted China's second attempt. The panel will now scrutinize India's trade commitments regarding these imported high-tech and solar energy items.
articles.raw_summary: China has successfully established a World Trade Organization panel to challenge India's solar cell, module, and IT goods policies. Beijing alleges these measures, including tariffs and incentives, unfairly target Chinese products. India had previously blocked the request, but the WTO body accepted China's second attempt. The panel will now scrutinize India's trade commitments regarding these imported high-tech and solar energy items.

articles.title: Oil prices fall as tankers exit Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Oil prices are falling as tankers resume passage through the Strait of Hormuz following a peace accord between the US, Israel, and Iran. This easing of supply concerns has pushed prices towards pre-war levels. The flow of oil is expected to continue, with nations coordinating to ensure smooth departures and discuss the strait's future management.
articles.raw_summary: Oil prices are dropping significantly as tankers resume passage through the Strait of Hormuz following a preliminary peace accord between the US, Israel, and Iran. This easing of supply concerns has pushed prices towards pre-war levels, surprising many analysts. Despite ongoing demining efforts, the flow of oil is expected to continue, with nations coordinating to ensure smooth departures and discuss the strait's future management.

articles.title: AI is an energy and water hog, here's what you can do to counter that

AI's growing demand is counterproductive to climate goals, with companies lacking transparency on resource use. Individuals can mitigate this by using AI less for simple tasks and making queries concise. While AI integration is pervasive, opting out and supporting eco-conscious search engines are viable choices for consumers to influence the market.

This trend is expected to continue, with data centers and advanced AI chips being major contributors to the surge in electricity demand.
articles.raw_summary: Artificial intelligence is transforming electricity into the world's most valuable resource, according to Bloom Energy founder KR Sridhar. Calling power the “invisible gold” of the AI age, he said the rapid growth of data centres and advanced AI chips is driving an unprecedented surge in electricity demand, making reliable and affordable power critical to economic growth and technological leadership.

articles.title: MoSPI releases discussion paper to identify apt method to compile coal monetary asset accounts
articles.summary: India's Ministry of Statistics has released a discussion paper on valuing coal assets monetarily, aligning with UN environmental accounting standards. articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India had issued draft Amendment Directions on  Net Open Position   Revised Instructions  on January 14, 2026 , seeking inputs from Regulated Entities (REs) and other stakeholders till February 3, 2026. The RBI's involvement in the celebration underscores the importance of supporting MSMEs in India.
articles.raw_summary: I am indeed very happy to be here among all of you as we enter the week which celebrates International MSME Day on 27th later in the week. articles.title: Fibre Traceability Systems Cannot Meet New Regulatory Evidence Standards, New Paper Argues
articles.summary: Fibre traceability systems built over two decades cannot meet evidence standards now being enforced under EU and US regulations, a new paper has revealed. Material identity is lost before tracking software records it, leaving most origin and recycled-content claims resting on paperwork rather than physical proof across every major fibre category.

articles.title: Europe's Textile EPR Systems Remain Highly Fragmented, Burdening Producers Operating Across Borders
articles.summary: Textile extended producer responsibility (EPR) systems across Europe remain highly fragmented, with no harmonised approach to registration, reporting or invoicing, according to a new analysis covering 12 Producer Responsibility Organisations across 11 countries. The findings identify priority areas for alignment and call for a common core of rules to ease the compliance load on producers.

articles.title: DRP 220626: Bodhghat Dam Faces Strong Tribal Resistance
articles.summary: Tribals in Bastar have revived protests against the proposed Bodhghat dam as fresh surveys begin. Thousands from 56 villages gathered, saying the project will displace communities and submerge sacred forests, hills and cultural sites. They argue that no compensation can replace this loss and warned strong resistance if the project moves forward. While the government says the dam will boost irrigation and development, locals see it as a threat to their land, identity and survival.

articles.title: June 2026: Mining Destabilizing Himalayan Rivers, Infrastructures in HP, UKD
articles.summary: A bridge on Malan river in Pauri district collapsed on July 12, 2023, amid flood surge in the river. Damage to the 300 m long bridge connecting Pauri with Kotdwar affected about 50,000 people. Locals and activists believed that the collapse was due to rampant illegal mining that had weakened the riverbed. They also reported that the riverbed mining started within 72 hours of bridge collapse. The locals were constantly reporting about illegal mining and sinking of their houses and farm land.

articles.title: India’s EV localisation gaining ground, but import dependence in chips and rare-earth magnets may hinder deeper value creation
articles.summary: New IEEFA and JMK Research report finds several EV component categories, excluding batteries, could reach 90–100% localisation in India by 2030, but deeper domestic value creation will depend on developing upstream capabilities in semiconductors, magnets, and advanced materials.
articles.raw_summary: New IEEFA and JMK Research report finds several EV component categories, excluding batteries, could reach 90–100% localisation in India by 2030, but deeper domestic value creation will depend on developing upstream capabilities in semiconductors, magnets, and advanced materials.

articles.title: Roofsol secures INR 260 crore from Aseem Infrastructure Finance for C&I solar expansion in India
articles.summary: Roofsol Energy has secured INR 260 crore in financing from Aseem Infrastructure Finance to expand its commercial and industrial (C&I) solar IPP portfolio in India.
articles.raw_summary: Roofsol Energy has secured INR 260 crore in financing from Aseem Infrastructure Finance to expand its commercial and industrial (C I) solar IPP portfolio in India.

articles.title: EMMVEE ramps up 1.4 GW TOPCon solar cell line with German equipment, places multi-GW follow-up order
articles.summary: Indian PV manufacturer EMMVEE has commissioned a 1.4 GW TOPCon solar cell production line, achieving cell efficiencies of up to 25.4% on M10 wafers. Following the ramp-up, German equipment suppliers involved in the project have secured follow-up orders from the company.

articles.title: Solarium Green wins EPC contract for 65 MW DC solar project in Maharashtra
articles.summary: Solarium Green Energy has secured a Letter of Award (LOA) for the engineering, procurement and construction (EPC) of a 50 MW AC (65 MW DC) solar PV project in Maharashtra.
articles.raw_summary: Solarium Green Energy has secured a Letter of Award (LOA) for the engineering, procurement and construction (EPC) of a 50 MW AC (65 MW DC) solar PV project in Maharashtra.

articles.title: Perovskite tandem PV for agrivoltaics
articles.summary: Researchers evaluated “farm-to-fork” life-cycle impacts of agrivoltaic lettuce systems using perovskite–silicon and perovskite–perovskite tandem PV, comparing them with conventional silicon PV across major US growing regions. The study finds that integrating advanced tandem solar with farming can reduce irrigation demand and significantly cut greenhouse gas emissions—potentially offsetting up to 30.9 million tons of CO₂ and saving around 8.4 billion m³ of water annually under favorable condition
articles.raw_summary: Researchers evaluated “farm-to-fork” life-cycle impacts of agrivoltaic lettuce systems using perovskite–silicon and perovskite–perovskite tandem PV, comparing them with conventional silicon PV across major US growing regions. The study finds that integrating advanced tandem solar with farming can reduce irrigation demand and significantly cut greenhouse gas emissions—potentially offsetting up to 30.9 million tons of CO₂ and saving around 8.4 billion m³ of water annually under favorable condition

articles.title: Moonwatt showcases sodium-ion storage solutions for utility-scale applications
articles.summary: Moonwatt unveiled a modular sodium-ion battery storage system for utility-scale PV plants at The Smarter E trade show in Munich, designed as distributed units placed across solar arrays instead of containerised blocks. The system uses passively cooled NFPP sodium-ion cells in DC- or AC-coupled configurations, targeting 93–94% solar-to-grid efficiency, lower balance-of-plant costs, and highly scalable, low-maintenance deployment without lithium, cobalt, or active cooling.
articles.raw_summary: Moonwatt unveiled a modular sodium-ion battery storage system for utility-scale PV plants at The Smarter E trade show in Munich, designed as distributed units placed across solar arrays instead of containerised blocks. articles.title: Ola Electric receives BIS certification for indigenous 46100 LFP battery cell
articles.summary: Ola Electric has become the first Indian company to receive Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) certification for an indigenously developed 46100-format lithium iron phosphate (LFP) battery cell. The certification expands the company's in-house cell portfolio beyond its nickel manganese cobalt (NMC) 4680 Bharat cell platform.
articles.raw_summary: Ola Electric has become the first Indian company to receive Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) certification for an indigenously developed 46100-format lithium iron phosphate (LFP) battery cell. The certification expands the company's in-house cell portfolio beyond its nickel manganese cobalt (NMC) 4680 Bharat cell platform.

articles.title: Jupiter International starts production at 1.25 GW TOPCon solar cell line in Himachal Pradesh
articles.summary: Jupiter International has begun production at its 1.25 GW TOPCon solar cell manufacturing line in Baddi, Himachal Pradesh. The site also houses 2 GW of monocrystalline PERC solar cell manufacturing capacity.
articles.raw_summary: Jupiter International has begun production at its 1.25 GW TOPCon solar cell manufacturing line in Baddi, Himachal Pradesh. The site also houses 2 GW of monocrystalline PERC solar cell manufacturing capacity.

articles.title: Global solar additions reached 664 GW in 2025
articles.summary: The world’s solar fleet surpassed 3 TW in early 2026 but with China forecast to install less solar this year than last, the global solar market appears on course for its first market contraction in over 20 years.
articles.raw_summary: The world’s solar fleet surpassed 3 TW in early 2026 but with China forecast to install less solar this year than last, the global solar market appears on course for its first market contraction in over 20 years.

articles.title: Indian Green Energy Stocks Show Mixed Performance As Sensex And Nifty End Higher On June 24
articles.summary: On June 24, 2026, the Indian stock market closed positively, with the S P BSE Sensex up 1.04% and Nifty 50 up 0.78%. Battery manufacturers like Exide Industries led gains, reflecting investor confidence in renewable energy. However, some companies, including Tata Power, faced declines, resulting in mixed sector performance overall. articles.summary: Kee Ming Group Berhad has secured a RM70 million subcontract for a large-scale solar power project in Kedah, Malaysia, through its subsidiary. The project involves extensive mechanical and electrical engineering works for a 99.99-megawatt solar plant. Work begins immediately, with full operations expected by February 2027. articles.summary: The Philippines will see a significant floating solar project worth $406 million, launched by SunAsia Energy and VinEnergo. articles.title: CAISO Surpasses Natural Gas as Solar Generation Surges in Early 2026
articles.summary: In California, utility-scale solar generation surpassed natural gas for the first time in early 2026, marking a significant transition in energy production. Solar output increased 21% while natural gas dropped by 60%. This shift, fueled by expanded solar capacity and battery storage, indicates a changing electricity landscape and growing reliance on renewables. The post CAISO Surpasses Natural Gas as Solar Generation Surges in Early 2026 appeared first on SolarQuarter .

articles.summary: To discuss the U.S. nuclear negotiations with Iran, Amna Nawaz spoke with Ernest Moniz. articles.title: A decade after Brexit, UK voters reflect on decision to break from European Union
articles.summary: Tuesday marks the 10th anniversary of the most divisive day in Britain's recent history: Brexit, the vote to leave the European Union. Voters were promised a Britain free of European laws, decreased immigration, and a major boost to business, without the EU's economic constraints. But as Malcolm Brabant reports, leave and remain voters have one thing in common. They're not celebrating.

articles.title: RBI says no to special exposure relaxations for state NBFCs
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has firmly rejected requests for special exemptions from concentration norms for government-owned NBFCs, emphasizing a principles-based regulatory approach. The central bank also denied calls to raise single counterparty limits for public sector enterprises. While allowing grandfathering of residual breaches and specific conditions for temporary limit breaches, the RBI has tightened rules to ensure regulatory clarity and stability.

articles.title: RBI tightens rules on recovery of excess pension payments
articles.summary: Government pensioners will now have stronger protection against unexpected deductions from their accounts. The Reserve Bank of India has mandated that banks must inform pensioners and obtain their consent before recovering any excess pension payments. This new directive ensures that individuals are aware and agree to any adjustments, preventing abrupt debits and requiring a written undertaking from pensioners for future recoveries.

articles.title: India has contingency plans in place amid 40% rainfall deficit, says Agriculture Commissioner
articles.summary: While India is currently facing 40 per cent rainfall deficit, the country has prepared district-level contingency plans to tackle possible rainfall uncertainty, with irrigation capacity and food grain reserves providing a buffer against any potential impact on crop production, said PK Singh, Agriculture Commissioner, Ministry of Agriculture   Farmers Welfare.

articles.title: RBI mandates kill switch for AI models at banks, introduces comprehensive model risk framework
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is implementing stringent rules for banks and financial entities using Artificial Intelligence. A new draft framework mandates 'kill switches' for AI models, ensuring immediate shutdown if errors occur. Banks must also ensure human oversight, disclose AI use to customers, and manage risks associated with third-party AI providers. Board-level accountability for AI governance is a key focus, with a risk-based approach to model oversight.

articles.title: India bonds gain, swap rates fall on dovish RBI comments, oil slide
articles.summary: Indian government bonds enjoyed a remarkable climb on Wednesday, highlighted by the 10-year yield experiencing its most significant drop in a month. Fueling this surge was a recent comment from Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra, who mentioned that contemplating interest rate hikes is premature. Additionally, decreasing oil prices contributed to positive market sentiment, driving yields down to three-month lows.

articles.summary: The Kerala High Court dismissed a public interest litigation (PIL) challenging the free bus travel scheme for women and transgender persons. articles.summary: The Congress party has called for making the right to vote a Fundamental Right, citing concerns about the Election Commission's impartiality. Tamil Nadu teacher recruitment scam explained: ED raids 18 locations, alleged manipulation
articles.summary: The ED carried out raids at 18 locations across Tamil Nadu as part of its investigation into the alleged teacher recruitment examination scam. The raids were conducted in Chennai, Coimbatore, Tiruchirappalli (Trichy) and Madurai .
articles.raw_summary: The ED carried out raids at 18 locations across Tamil Nadu as part of its investigation into the alleged teacher recruitment examination scam. The raids were conducted in Chennai, Coimbatore, Tiruchirappalli (Trichy) and Madurai .

articles.title: IndiaAI backing could make government a minority shareholder in Sarvam
articles.summary: The central government may become a minority shareholder in AI startup Sarvam as part of its $300 million funding round. This is due to non-cash support provided under the IndiaAI Mission. The government's backing could give it a 1-2% stake in the $1.5 billion-valued startup.
articles.raw_summary: The central government could end up with a 1-2% stake in AI startup Sarvam, valued at $1.5 billion, as part of its $300 million funding round. This rare instance of equity holding arises from non-cash support provided under the IndiaAI Mission, where the government offered compute infrastructure in exchange for compulsorily convertible debentures.

articles.title: Tech entrepreneurs seeking the next AI frontier are pivoting from chatbots to 'world models'
articles.summary: Researchers are shifting their focus from text-based chatbots to 'world models' in AI, which aim to understand and interact with the physical world. This shift is attracting significant investment and could lead to smarter robots and more immersive virtual environments.
articles.raw_summary: AI is evolving beyond text-based chatbots, with researchers now focusing on "world models." These advanced systems aim to teach AI to understand and interact with the physical world, much like humans do. This shift is attracting significant investment, with a new generation of entrepreneurs and prominent scientists exploring how AI can learn spatial and temporal structures, paving the way for smarter robots and more immersive virtual environments.

articles.title: Agility Robotics heads to Wall Street in a $2.5 billion bet on staffing warehouses with humanoids
articles.summary: Agility Robotics, a company that develops humanoid robots for warehouse operations, plans to go public with a $2.5 billion valuation. Their 'Digit' robots are already deployed in various industrial environments. This move signifies a noteworthy advancement for the rapidly growing humanoid robot industry.
articles.raw_summary: Agility Robotics, an AI-driven humanoid robotics for warehouse operations, plans to go public with a remarkable $2.5 billion valuation. This move signifies a noteworthy advancement for the rapidly growing humanoid robot industry. Their 'Digit' robots, optimized for monotonous manual tasks, are already deployed in various industrial environments, supported by leading technology firms.

articles.title: Apple-Intel chip deal makes strategic sense but production is years away
articles.summary: Apple is reportedly partnering with Intel for chip manufacturing due to TSMC's inability to meet surging AI demand. However, production is expected to take two to three years. This development may have implications for the global tech industry, but it does not directly affect Indian policy.
articles.raw_summary: Apple is reportedly turning to Intel for chip manufacturing, a move driven by TSMC's inability to meet surging AI demand and Apple's need for more capacity. This partnership, however, faces significant hurdles, with analysts predicting it could take two to three years for advanced chips to be ready. Intel's past struggles with chip yields and quality add to the commercial and financial risks involved in this ambitious collaboration.

articles.title: Maharashtra CM meets Amazon CEO, invites company to invest in data centres, healthcare
articles.summary: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis met Amazon CEO Andy Jassy to discuss investment opportunities in the state's data centres, healthcare, and AI-driven skill development. The meeting aims to boost Maharashtra's economy and leverage AI for public services. Fadnavis highlighted the state's ambition to double its economy by 2030.
articles.raw_summary: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis met Amazon CEO Andy Jassy, urging investment in the state's data centres, healthcare, and AI-driven skill development. Fadnavis highlighted Maharashtra's ambition to double its economy by 2030 and its commitment to leveraging AI for public services. The state is fostering a robust ecosystem for tech expansion, including green data centres and the Medicity project, seeking partnerships with Amazon to boost MSMEs through AI skilling.

articles.title: Microsoft's quantum computing technology called into question, again
articles.summary: Microsoft's quantum computing technology has been questioned by a new scientific critique in Nature. This could impact the company's 2029 target. The technology is still in its development stage, and Microsoft maintains that its research is progressing practically.
articles.raw_summary: A new scientific critique in Nature casts doubt on Microsoft's quantum computing claims, potentially impacting its 2029 target. While rivals pursue established methods, Microsoft's long-term bet on a novel technology faces renewed skepticism. The company maintains its research is progressing practically, despite past retractions and current concerns over identifying a crucial subatomic particle and a minute gap in conductive wires.

articles.title: Swiggy Instamart COO Ankit Jain, CBO Hari Kumar quit amid top-level rejig
articles.summary: Swiggy's Instamart has seen key leadership departures, including COO Ankit Jain and CBO Hari Kumar, due to personal reasons. This comes as the company faces intense competition in the quick commerce sector. The changes may impact the company's operations and employment.
articles.raw_summary: Quick commerce platform Instamart has seen key leadership departures with COO Ankit Jain and CBO Hari Kumar resigning due to personal reasons. Both executives, formerly of Flipkart, played crucial roles in the quick commerce business. This comes as Swiggy, Instamart's parent company, faces intense competition in the rapidly growing quick commerce sector, with other players aggressively expanding their reach.

articles.title: Meta, Microsoft lead $850 billion boom in data center leases
articles.summary: Tech giants Meta and Microsoft are investing heavily in data center leases, which will require significant amounts of energy. This has implications for the tech industry's energy consumption and may lead to policy discussions around sustainable energy practices.
articles.raw_summary: Tech giants Meta and Microsoft are pouring billions into future data center leases, pushing industry-wide commitments past $850 billion. Meta alone pledged an additional $79 billion, signaling massive AI infrastructure investment. Microsoft also added over $41 billion, despite capacity constraints. This surge highlights sustained, long-term spending on crucial tech infrastructure like semiconductors and energy.

articles.title: Beyond the top 3, Amazon and Flipkart wage war to win the quick commerce crown
articles.summary: Amazon and Flipkart are aggressively expanding their quick commerce operations to capture market share from established players. This move is aimed at increasing their presence in tier 2 and 3 cities. The expansion is expected to intensify competition in the quick commerce space.
articles.raw_summary: Ecommerce giants Amazon and Flipkart are aggressively expanding their quick commerce operations, aiming to capture market share from established players like Blinkit, Instamart, and Zepto. Flipkart Minutes is rapidly growing its fulfilment centres, focusing on tier 2 and 3 cities, while Amazon Now plans to extend its dark store network to 300 cities.

articles.title: Premier says China's tech advancements an 'opportunity' for world, not threat
articles.summary: China's Premier Li Qiang has said that China's tech advancements are an opportunity for the world, not a threat. He attributed China's success to a vast domestic market and significant corporate investment. This statement may have implications for India's tech industry and foreign policy.
articles.raw_summary: China's Premier Li Qiang has countered global concerns about the nation's technological surge, framing it as a worldwide opportunity, not a threat. He dismissed claims that hefty state subsidies are the primary driver of China's high-tech boom, attributing its success to a vast domestic market and significant corporate investment. Li highlighted advancements in areas like EVs and AI, suggesting they offer empowerment rather than disruption to global economies.

articles.title: Walmart's Flipkart, Amazon step up India 'quick commerce' bet as competition heats up
articles.summary: Walmart's Flipkart and Amazon are accelerating their quick commerce expansion in India, targeting smaller cities to compete with domestic players. This rapid delivery sector, promising goods within minutes, is reshaping shopping habits. While Blinkit and Swiggy lead in store count, Flipkart and Amazon are significantly increasing their reach, with Flipkart focusing on value-conscious customers in smaller towns and Amazon broadening its ultra-fast delivery to 300 cities.

This rapid delivery sector, promising goods within minutes, is reshaping shopping habits. While Blinkit and Swiggy lead in store count, Flipkart and Amazon are significantly increasing their reach, with Flipkart focusing on value-conscious customers in smaller towns and Amazon broadening its ultra-fast delivery to 300 cities.

articles.title: South Korea's SK Hynix says to raise $29 billion in ADR listing
articles.summary: South Korea's SK Hynix is planning a record-breaking secondary listing on the U.S. stock market, aiming to raise up to $29.4 billion. This move capitalizes on strong investor demand for AI stocks, with the memory chip giant seeking to expand its capacity and gain access to U.S. investors. The company, a key supplier to Nvidia, has seen its shares surge this year, outperforming rivals.
articles.raw_summary: South Korea's SK Hynix is planning a record-breaking secondary listing on the U.S. stock market, aiming to raise up to $29.4 billion. This move capitalizes on strong investor demand for AI stocks, with the memory chip giant seeking to expand its capacity and gain access to U.S. investors. The company, a key supplier to Nvidia, has seen its shares surge this year, outperforming rivals.

articles.title: Chinese EV makers are shut out of India - but their tech isn't
articles.summary: Despite India's restrictions on Chinese automakers, their electric vehicle technology is finding its way into the Indian market. Tata Motors and JSW Motor are partnering with China's Chery, utilizing their platforms for local EV production. These deals, structured as supply arrangements without technology transfer, aim to boost India's manufacturing while allowing Chinese firms to expand revenue amidst domestic slowdowns.
articles.raw_summary: Despite India's restrictions on Chinese automakers, their electric vehicle technology is finding its way into the Indian market. Tata Motors and JSW Motor are partnering with China's Chery, utilizing their platforms for local EV production. These deals, structured as supply arrangements without technology transfer, aim to boost India's manufacturing while allowing Chinese firms to expand revenue amidst domestic slowdowns.

articles.title: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy visits India; to meet PM Modi on Thursday
articles.summary: The visit comes as Amazon sharpens its focus on quick commerce in the country. The company announced that it is expanding its 10-minute delivery service, Amazon Now, to 300 cities. Currently, Amazon Now has about 500 dark stores across 15 metro and non-metro cities, including Bengaluru, Delhi-NCR, Mumbai, Pune, Hyderabad, Amritsar, and Kochi.

articles.title: Singling out Huawei, China's premier defends tech rise, rejects subsidy claims
articles.summary: China's Premier Li Qiang defended the country's tech advancements, attributing them to firm investments rather than state subsidies. He cited Huawei's resilience against foreign restrictions. This development may lead to concerns about Chinese state support and its impact on critical technologies in Europe, similar to US concerns.
articles.raw_summary: China's Premier Li Qiang has pushed back against claims that state subsidies fuel the nation's tech advancements in areas like AI and EVs. Speaking at a World Economic Forum summit, he attributed China's innovation to substantial firm investments, citing Huawei's resilience against foreign restrictions. Analysts warn of a potential European trade front mirroring US concerns over Chinese state support and its impact on critical technologies.

articles.title: Qualcomm in talks to provide custom chip-design services to ByteDance
articles.summary: US chip giant Qualcomm is reportedly in talks with Chinese tech behemoth ByteDance, parent of TikTok, to design custom chips. This move signals Qualcomm's bid to diversify beyond its core smartphone market and highlights continued US-China tech business engagement despite geopolitical tensions. The potential deal could involve video processing units, leveraging Qualcomm's recent acquisition of AlphaWave Semi. While discussions are ongoing, the outcome remains uncertain.

This move signals Qualcomm's bid to diversify beyond its core smartphone market and highlights continued US-China tech business engagement despite geopolitical tensions. The potential deal could involve video processing units, leveraging Qualcomm's recent acquisition of AlphaWave Semi. While discussions are ongoing, the outcome remains uncertain.

articles.title: Meta’s Chris Cox sought Kunal Shah’s WhatsApp advice - then made him leader
articles.summary: Meta Chief Product Officer Chris Cox reached out to Shah directly in the spring, seeking advice on picking the future leader of WhatsApp. Cox had been calling entrepreneurs and investors in places like India, Brazil and Mexico, countries where WhatsApp is a dominant part of business and culture, and Shah’s ideas about the future of the app left an immediate impression.

articles.title: Alibaba sues Pentagon over blacklist designation
articles.summary: Alibaba Group has filed a federal lawsuit in the US against the Pentagon's designation of the company as a 'Chinese military company'. The lawsuit argues that the labeling is arbitrary and lacks due process. This development has significant implications for the US-China trade relationship.
articles.raw_summary: Alibaba Group on Tuesday filed a federal lawsuit challenging the US Defense Department's designation of the Chinese technology giant as a "Chinese military company," arguing the labeling is arbitrary and lacks due process. The lawsuit, filed Tuesday in San Jose federal court, contests the Pentagon's determination that Alibaba qualifies for inclusion on the federal military company list.

articles.title: Zuckerberg bets on Kunal Shah for WhatsApp’s aggressive financial services, AI push
articles.summary: Meta's WhatsApp is set for a major financial services and AI push with Kunal Shah at the helm. This move aims to leverage AI agents and the Meta AI chatbot to drive growth, particularly in India, a key market with over half a billion users. Shah's appointment signals India's growing influence on WhatsApp's global strategy, potentially reviving its fintech ambitions.

articles.title: Beauty's back-end gets investor attention; Naturis in talks to raise Rs 100 crore
articles.summary: Investors are increasingly backing India's beauty and personal care manufacturing sector, moving beyond consumer brands. Companies like Naturis Cosmetics and NG Electro Products are attracting significant capital as new-age brands scale and outsource production. This shift signals a maturing ecosystem, with manufacturers offering crucial formulation and innovation support, poised to benefit from the sector's projected growth.

articles.title: AI risks spark 30% surge in India cybersecurity spends as enterprises ramp up defences
articles.summary: Corporate India is witnessing a significant surge in cybersecurity spending, up 30% year-on-year, as businesses race to safeguard data and AI systems. Even traditionally less-invested sectors like manufacturing are digitizing aggressively. This trend, driven by AI's expanding attack surface and increasing regulatory pressure from bodies like CERT-In, is making cybersecurity a crucial, ongoing operational expense for enterprises.

articles.title: Flipkart Minutes expands to 1,000 stores as quick commerce competition intensifies
articles.summary: Flipkart's quick commerce arm, Minutes, is rapidly expanding, aiming for 1,500 fulfilment centres in over 180 cities soon. This aggressive growth, adding about 100 stores monthly, targets both metros and smaller markets, differentiating from competitors like Blinkit, Zepto, and Instamart. Minutes is leveraging Flipkart's vast customer base to offer a broader assortment beyond groceries, including mobiles and electronics, to capture a larger market share.

articles.title: Mark Zuckerberg directed Meta to create a prediction markets app: NYT
articles.summary: Meta is reportedly developing a new app called 'Arena' that will function like prediction markets. The app will initially use a points system and may later allow real-money wagering. This move taps into the growing popularity of prediction markets, which have seen significant interest during the U.S. presidential election.
articles.raw_summary: Meta is reportedly developing a new app, codenamed 'Arena,' designed to function like prediction markets such as Polymarket and Kalshi. Initially, it will likely use a points system, with the possibility of real-money wagering later. This move taps into the growing popularity of prediction markets, which have seen significant interest during the U.S. presidential election.

articles.summary: Kunal Shah, the founder of Cred, has been appointed as the new CEO of WhatsApp. This move has sparked discussion on social media, with industry leaders welcoming the leadership transition. The appointment may have implications for India's technology and governance policies.

articles.title: Foreign influence increasing over India's fintech sector: GTRI on Meta's investment in Cred
articles.summary: Think tank GTRI said that the deal gives Meta a foothold in one of the country's richest repositories of consumer financial-behaviour data, covering credit cards, loans, investments, insurance, and UPI transactions. On the other hand, it said China built its digital payments ecosystem through domestic champions such as Alipay and WeChat Pay, keeping ownership and strategic control within Chinese companies.

articles.title: UK tribunal gives go ahead for $4 billion lawsuit against Apple over iCloud services
articles.summary: A UK tribunal has allowed a $4 billion lawsuit against Apple over its iCloud service. The lawsuit claims Apple has manipulated its market position, trapping users. This decision empowers UK iPhone users to seek damages, with the trial scheduled for 2028.

articles.title: Ola Electric gets BIS certification for indigenous LFP battery cell
articles.summary: Ola Electric's subsidiary has secured Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) certification for its indigenously developed 46100 lithium iron phosphate (LFP) battery cell. This marks a first for an Indian company in this cylindrical format. The new cell, designed for both electric mobility and energy storage, expands their in-house battery portfolio and aims to reduce reliance on imported technologies.

articles.summary: Apple's incoming CEO, John Ternus, plans to strengthen the company's design-led culture as AI reshapes the technology industry. The court's decision may lead to changes in how companies approach diversity and inclusion in their hiring practices.

articles.title: El Niño is set to further disrupt India's $300 billion farm supply chain
articles.summary: India's crucial monsoon rains are delayed, disrupting the nation's $300 billion farm economy just as planting season begins. This agricultural setback, exacerbated by a developing El Niño, threatens staple crops like rice, cotton, and soybeans, impacting prices and potentially fueling inflation. While India's food system shows resilience, worsening climate conditions pose a growing challenge to this vital agricultural lifeline.

articles.title: RBI in wait-and-watch mode, no certainty on rate hike: Governor Sanjay Malhotra
articles.summary: Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra stated that an interest rate hike is premature, as inflation hasn't broadly impacted consumer prices. The central bank remains in a 'wait-and-watch' mode, closely monitoring global uncertainties and the monsoon's progress. While acknowledging improved outlooks, risks persist, but India is well-prepared for challenges. The RBI has revised inflation forecasts upwards and growth forecasts downwards for the fiscal year.

articles.title: S&P cuts India's FY27 growth forecast to 6.6%, sees inflation at 5.1%
articles.summary: India's economic growth is projected to slow to 6.6% in 2026-27 due to energy pressures, a weaker monsoon, and global slowdown, S P Global Ratings reported. Retail inflation is expected to climb to 5.1% this fiscal year. Despite near-term challenges, GDP growth is anticipated to rebound to 7.2% in 2027-28, with oil prices expected to stabilize.

articles.title: Goyal heads to UK ahead of India-UK trade pact rollout
articles.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal's UK visit from June 25-27 precedes the July 15 implementation of the India-UK CETA and Double Contribution Convention. Discussions with UK's Business and Trade Secretary Peter Kyle will focus on regulatory alignment, customs coordination, and operationalizing tariff liberalisation for Indian exports. The talks also aim to boost professional mobility and achieve a bilateral trade target of $120 billion by 2030.

articles.title: India, US inch closer to interim trade pact after fresh talks
articles.summary: India and the US are nearing an interim trade deal, with both nations reporting significant progress in recent negotiations. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal and US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer concluded two days of talks, aiming to finalize the first phase of a bilateral trade agreement. Discussions focused on market access, digital trade, and non-tariff barriers, with both sides committed to a balanced pact delivering tangible benefits.

articles.title: Exporters urge govt not to impose curbs on onions
articles.summary: Indian onion exporters are urging the government against imposing export curbs, citing a surge in wholesale prices at Lasalgaon market. Delayed sowing and adverse weather have impacted production, pushing prices up. Exporters warn that restrictions could jeopardize markets painstakingly regained after previous bans, potentially shifting buyers to competing nations and damaging India's trade reputation.

articles.title: Goyal meets exporters to discuss ways to boost shipments, leverage FTAs
articles.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal met with exporters to strategize on boosting outbound shipments and maximizing opportunities through Free Trade Agreements. Discussions focused on strengthening MSME support, fostering innovation, improving export facilitation, and expanding India's global value chain role. The meeting reviewed strategies to enhance the export ecosystem, diversify markets, and boost trade competitiveness, with councils presenting their progress on key performance indicators.

articles.title: India targets $100 billion textile exports by 2030, focus on sustainability, FTAs
articles.summary: India is aiming to become a global textile powerhouse, targeting $100 billion in exports by 2030. The strategy emphasizes sustainability, including recycling and environmental compliance, alongside leveraging free trade agreements. Key initiatives involve digital product passports, supporting MSMEs, and enhancing product-market strategies. The focus is on translating policy into action across all levels to boost the sector and promote 'Brand India' internationally.

articles.title: Service output index to debut next month with FY25 base
articles.summary: India is set to launch the Index of Service Production (ISP) next month, a crucial new monthly indicator mirroring the Industrial Production Index. This initiative aims to precisely track the growth of the vital services sector, which forms the backbone of the Indian economy. The index will initially cover key areas like trade, transport, and finance, with health and education to be included later.

articles.title: Russia and India working on increasing bilateral trade volumes: Kremlin spokesman
articles.summary: Russia and India are actively boosting bilateral trade, aiming for a $100 billion target by 2030, up from the current $60 billion. Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov highlighted the "notably privileged partnership" and steady uptrend in economic relations. He praised India as a populous, fast-growing economy with immense potential, underscoring the shared commitment to enhancing cooperation across various international platforms.

articles.title: NHAI intensifies drive against illegal highway parking
articles.summary: The National Highways Authority of India is intensifying efforts to clear illegal parking and roadside encroachments on national highways, identified as major safety hazards. Focusing on 595 critical spots nationwide, NHAI is collaborating with state authorities to remove these obstructions. Additionally, the authority is ensuring all traffic management systems are operational and enhancing emergency response with more ambulances and patrols for safer commutes.

articles.title: India to release first-ever monthly index tracking services sector output from July 14
articles.summary: India is launching a new Index of Services Production (ISP) to track the services sector's growth, mirroring the Index of Industrial Production. This crucial macroeconomic indicator, with 2024-25 as the base year, will be released monthly with a 60-day lag, starting with April 2026 data on July 14. The ISP aims to provide high-frequency insights into the dominant services sector's performance, complementing existing economic data.

Both sides expressed appreciation for the constructive discussions, highlighting a forward-looking approach to deepening the bilateral partnership.

This collaboration significantly boosts India's energy security and fosters economic growth, with both nations committed to further deepening these ties.

articles.summary: Hyderabad Metro's future is set for a new direction as the Centre and Telangana government have agreed to appoint SBI Capital Markets. The decision follows high-level discussions, signaling a collaborative approach to boost the city's metro network.

articles.title: Govt draws up contingency plans for 315 districts amid 42% rain deficit
articles.summary: India faces a significant 42% rainfall deficit this monsoon, exacerbated by El Nino. The government is implementing contingency plans across 315 vulnerable districts, focusing on water conservation and crop diversification. While sowing is slightly ahead of last year, soybean planting lags. District-specific strategies are being deployed to mitigate potential impacts on farm output and ensure food security.

articles.summary: India and the US are pushing for an interim trade pact, with high-level talks led by Piyush Goyal and Jamieson Greer aiming for a deal before July 24. Discussions focused on advancing negotiations for a balanced and mutually beneficial agreement, building on a February joint statement.

articles.title: India's private sector growth slows to three-month low in June as demand weakens, costs rise: HSBC PMI
articles.summary: India's private sector experienced a slowdown in June, reaching a three-month low as rising costs and softening demand impacted business. The composite PMI dipped to 57.4. While manufacturing output growth eased, both manufacturing and services PMIs saw declines. Despite this, businesses remain hopeful for future output, though overall confidence has weakened, with manufacturers showing the lowest sentiment in nearly four years.

articles.title: India to resume train coach exports to Bangladesh, first shipment since Sheikh Hasina's ouster likely in July
articles.summary: India is set to export a train rake of up to 20 coaches to Bangladesh next month, marking a significant resumption of trade. This shipment, manufactured at the Rail Coach Factory in Kapurthala, is part of a larger contract for 200 broad-gauge passenger coaches funded by the European Investment Bank. Despite a political delay, the project is now back on track, with RITES overseeing the supply, commissioning, and warranty.

articles.title: India prepares contingency plans for 315 districts as monsoon falters
articles.summary: India is implementing contingency plans for over 300 vulnerable districts facing a potentially weak monsoon. With rains currently 43% below average and a forecast for continued weakness, the government is urging farmers to adopt short-duration, water-efficient crops. Preparations include repairing water-harvesting structures to mitigate the impact on crucial summer-sown crops and rural incomes.

articles.title: India launches anti-dumping probe into electrical steel imports from China, 3 other nations
articles.summary: India has launched an anti-dumping investigation into electrical steel imports from China, Japan, Korea, and Russia. This move, prompted by a complaint from JSW JFE Electrical Steel Nashik, aims to protect domestic manufacturers from alleged cheap imports that are reportedly causing significant harm. The probe will determine if dumping has occurred and if duties are necessary to ensure fair trade.

articles.title: Banks can offer loans for FCNR (B), rebook deposits, RBI clarifies
articles.summary: Indian banks can now lend against FCNR(B) deposits, with the RBI offering forex swaps only on the principal amount. This move aims to attract dollars by absorbing currency hedging costs for banks, boosting FCNR(B) rates to 6-7.1%. The central bank also clarified rules for stand-by letters of credit and introduced a swap facility for external commercial borrowings, easing dollar hedging for businesses.

articles.title: RBI clears loans, SBLCs against FCNR(B) deposits under swap scheme
articles.summary: Banks can now offer loans to non-residents and issue standby letters of credit against FCNR(B) deposits under the RBI's swap facility. This regulatory clarity from the central bank allows Indian banks, including overseas branches, to lend to non-residents who then place these funds as FCNR(B) deposits, serving as collateral. This move aims to boost foreign currency inflows, with the swap facility covering only the principal amount of deposits.

articles.summary: India's trade saw a healthy 5.4% growth in Q4 FY26, driven by robust services exports that significantly boosted the services trade surplus to $60.4 billion. Despite a dip in merchandise exports, the strong performance in services, which are increasingly closing the gap with goods exports, bolstered overall trade figures. India also solidified its global standing as a leading services exporter.

articles.summary: A significant India-UK trade pact, set to take effect on July 15, promises to boost bilateral ties and open a market exceeding $500 billion for Indian businesses. While hailed as a landmark achievement, British MP Bob Blackman cautioned that future UK-EU trade deals could potentially diminish the benefits of this agreement. Both sides acknowledge further work is needed to achieve friction-free trade.

articles.title: India needs 7-8% growth for Viksit Bharat, private investment and export push crucial: EAC-PM Chairman
articles.summary: India needs sustained 7-8% economic growth to become 'Viksit Bharat' by 2047, driven by private investment and exports. Structural reforms are in place, with 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' focusing on enhancing domestic capability and product quality for global competitiveness, not import substitution. The nation is also prioritizing organic farming to reduce fertilizer subsidies and has sufficient food reserves to manage inflation, despite geopolitical risks.

articles.title: GST wins India Inc's vote, but businesses seek faster refunds and smarter reforms: Deloitte
articles.summary: India's GST regime has achieved widespread acceptance, with digitisation and rate adjustments hailed as major successes. Businesses now prioritize clearer interpretations, uniform audits, and faster refunds to ease working capital. The corporate sector is eager for AI-powered compliance tools and a unified taxpayer dashboard, signaling a push for more advanced, business-friendly reforms.

articles.title: Gold extends losses on Fed tightening outlook, dollar strength
articles.summary: Gold prices have fallen due to a strong dollar and anticipation of Federal Reserve interest rate hikes. This has led to a decrease in gold prices, nearing $3,985 per ounce. The situation is being closely watched by investors, who are awaiting key U.S. inflation data for further monetary policy direction.

Traders are factoring in multiple rate increases this year, with a significant chance of a September hike. Investors are now keenly awaiting key U.S. inflation data for further monetary policy direction.

articles.summary: The US dollar is experiencing a significant surge, reaching multi-month highs against major currencies like the euro and yen. This strength is fueled by expectations of a robust US economy and higher interest rates. The dollar's upward momentum may continue in the short term due to corporate demand.

This weakness stemmed from a strengthening dollar index, fueled by expectations of a US Federal Reserve interest rate hike later this year, pushing the DXY to a 13-month high.

Automobile and IT sectors also experienced significant selling pressure, with concerns over oil prices and AI's impact on tech revenues cited as key reasons.

articles.title: RBI eases forex exposure rules, giving banks more room to trade
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has updated foreign exchange exposure rules for banks. These changes simplify calculations by merging onshore and offshore positions and allow inclusion of overseas earnings. Crucially, banks can now exclude certain long-term foreign currency investments, potentially boosting trading capacity. Forex risk capital will be based on actual net open positions, with gold treated separately.

articles.title: Weak monsoon raises food inflation concerns, but rate hike seen unlikely
articles.summary: Economists are split on how a weak monsoon will affect food prices, with some believing falling oil prices will offset any hikes. Others warn of El Nino's impact on sowing and food costs. The Reserve Bank of India is unlikely to raise interest rates soon, preferring to wait for clarity on the monsoon's agricultural and inflation effects.

articles.summary: Zerodha co-founder Nithin Kamath shared his past entanglement with a pyramid scheme in his late teens, emphasizing that wealth building requires no shortcuts. He warned against schemes promising unusually high returns, noting that many continue to operate in India despite growing financial awareness. Kamath cautioned that making money in the stock market isn't easy and urged people to avoid referral-based money-making schemes, calling them almost invariably fraudulent.

articles.title: ITAT: Accrued interest component on  non-convertible debentures sales is taxable as interest income
articles.summary: The ITAT has ruled that accrued interest on non-convertible debentures sales is taxable as interest income. This decision may lead to greater scrutiny and fresh litigation. Tax experts say the ruling could have significant implications for investors and companies involved in debenture sales.

Tax experts say the decision could trigger greater scrutiny and fresh litigation.

Analysts attribute this slump to signals from the Federal Reserve and a notable downturn in technology stocks. Investors are now closely monitoring upcoming US inflation figures to gauge the next steps in the market.

articles.title: IRFC OFS subscribed over 1.5x by institutional investors; govt to exercise greenshoe option
articles.summary: The Indian government's offer for sale in Indian Railway Finance Corporation (IRFC) received strong demand from institutional investors. As a result, the Centre will sell an additional 1% stake in IRFC. This move is significant for the government's efforts to raise funds and support the railway sector.
articles.raw_summary: The government’s offer for sale in IRFC received a strong response from institutional investors, with the non-retail portion subscribed 1.86 times on Wednesday. Following the robust demand, the Centre will exercise the greenshoe option, allowing it to sell an additional 1% stake in the railway financing company through the OFS route.

articles.title: RBI finalises digital banking fraud protection rules; introduces shadow reversal, extends relief to sole proprietors
articles.summary: Victims of digital payment fraud losing up to Rs 50,000 can now get up to Rs 25,000 compensation from banks, effective January 1, 2027. The RBI will fund 65% of this, with banks contributing the rest. Sole proprietors are now covered, and cross-border frauds are included. Reporting within five days to the cybercrime portal and bank is crucial for eligibility.

articles.title: Brent crude hits lowest since before start of Mideast war as more tankers exit Hormuz
articles.summary: Global oil prices fell due to increased supply and a forecast cut by J.P. Morgan. The Strait of Hormuz is clearing, and an interim deal with Iran is unlocking stored crude. This may boost sales, but uncertainty remains over the durability of the U.S.-Iran accord.
articles.raw_summary: Global oil prices plunged Wednesday, hitting multi-month lows as supply worries eased with tankers clearing the Strait of Hormuz. An interim deal with Iran is unlocking stored crude, potentially boosting sales significantly. While U.S. inventories remain tight, rising fuel stockpiles and a forecast cut by J.P. Morgan also contributed to the price drop. Uncertainty, however, lingers over the durability of the U.S.-Iran accord.

articles.title: Quote of the day by David Abrams: "There are good assets and bad assets but good prices and bad prices supersede whether the assets are good or bad"
articles.summary: David Abrams, a value investor, emphasizes the importance of entry price over asset quality in determining investment outcomes. He suggests that even strong businesses can underperform if overvalued, while weaker ones can generate returns if bought cheaply. This insight reinforces core value investing principles.

articles.title: JP Morgan lowers Brent crude price forecast for second-half 2026
articles.summary: J.P. Morgan has lowered its forecast for Brent crude oil prices in the second half of 2026 due to weaker inventory draws and declining oil demand. The bank now estimates Brent prices will average $86 in Q3 and $80 in Q4. This update may have implications for the global energy market, but it does not directly affect Indian policy.
articles.raw_summary: J.P. Morgan has updated its Brent crude oil price forecast for 2026, lowering expectations due to unexpectedly weaker inventory draws and a decline in oil demand. The bank now estimates Brent prices will average $86 in Q3 and $80 in Q4, finishing the year at $78. This downward revision arises from private operators maintaining stock levels, making government releases critical for refinery operations.

This ambitious expansion positions Adani as a key player in India's evolving infrastructure landscape.

articles.title: A decade after Brexit, Britain is still counting the economic cost

articles.title: India must rapidly boost electricity supply to stay competitive in AI, says Bloom Energy founder
articles.summary: India could face significant economic costs if it fails to quickly expand electricity generation to support the rapid growth of AI and data centres, according to Bloom Energy founder KR Sridhar. articles.title: Oil prices could fall below $60 by 2027 as supply outpaces demand, says energy expert
articles.summary: Christof Ruehl, an energy expert, predicts that oil prices could fall below $60 per barrel by 2027 due to a surplus in supply. This is because energy markets have become more resilient, and efficiency gains and the rise of electric vehicles could bring the world closer to peak oil demand.
articles.raw_summary: Christof Ruehl, Global Advisor at Crystol Energy, expects oil supply to exceed demand by more than 1 million barrels per day in 2027, potentially pushing prices below $60 per barrel. Ruehl also said energy markets have become more resilient, while efficiency gains and the rise of electric vehicles could bring the world closer to peak oil demand.

articles.title: ECB secures key parliamentary backing for digital euro
articles.summary: The European Central Bank has secured key parliamentary backing for the digital euro, an electronic wallet guaranteed by the central bank. This will allow all euro zone residents to make payments online and in person. The digital euro is a concept relevant to European finance, but its implications for India are indirect.
articles.raw_summary: The digital euro, essentially an electronic wallet guaranteed by the central bank but marketed by banks or fintech companies, will allow all euro zone residents to make payments online and in person.

articles.title: Meta's CRED deal raises fears over who controls Indians' financial data
articles.summary: A report by GTRI has raised concerns that Meta's proposed investment in CRED could deepen the influence of foreign technology companies over India's fintech sector and consumer financial data. The think tank has called for greater transparency on data-sharing safeguards and regulatory oversight of the deal.
articles.raw_summary: A report by GTRI has raised concerns that Meta's proposed investment in CRED could deepen the influence of foreign technology companies over India's fintech sector and consumer financial data. The think tank has called for greater transparency on data-sharing safeguards and regulatory oversight of the deal.

articles.title: AI trainer emerges as world's fastest-growing tech job as companies race to deploy AI
articles.summary: A new study by Randstad Digital shows that companies are shifting from experimenting with AI to putting it to work. Demand for AI trainers, AI solutions leads and automation specialists is surging, while India now accounts for more than one-fifth of all AI job openings globally.
articles.raw_summary: A new study by Randstad Digital shows that companies are shifting from experimenting with AI to putting it to work. Demand for AI trainers, AI solutions leads and automation specialists is surging, while India now accounts for more than one-fifth of all AI job openings globally.

articles.title: What is liquid cooling? Nvidia’s big bet to solve AI data centres' growing heat and water problem
articles.summary: Nvidia is exploring liquid cooling as a solution to reduce heat and water usage in data centers. This technology could potentially be used in data centers worldwide, but it does not have any direct policy implications for India at this time.
articles.raw_summary: Nvidia said that liquid cooling could significantly reduce water use in data centres.

articles.title: Global markets offer a warning for India’s data centre industry
articles.summary: Unlike many mature markets, demand growth in India is not being driven solely by global hyperscalers. Rapid digitalisation, AI adoption, growth in global capability centres, increasing cloud penetration, fintech expansion and enterprise digital transformation are all creating structural demand for data infrastructure, writes global real estate investor and Capital Advisor Chanakya Chakravarti.
articles.raw_summary: Unlike many mature markets, demand growth in India is not being driven solely by global hyperscalers. Rapid digitalisation, AI adoption, growth in global capability centres, increasing cloud penetration, fintech expansion and enterprise digital transformation are all creating structural demand for data infrastructure, writes global real estate investor and Capital Advisor Chanakya Chakravarti.

articles.title: Water stress in Taiwan's chip heartland raises fresh supply chain concerns: Moody's
articles.summary: Moody's warned that water reliability is becoming an increasingly important factor for economic resilience in regions with high-value manufacturing industries, including Taiwan's chip heartland.
articles.raw_summary: Moody's warned that water reliability is becoming an increasingly important factor for economic resilience, industrial continuity and credit quality in regions that host high-value manufacturing industries.

articles.title: CATL launches battery storage system using new sodium technology
articles.summary: CATL, a Chinese company, has launched a new battery storage system using sodium technology. This system is expected to be more cost-effective and have a better supply chain compared to lithium batteries. The system is initially being delivered in China and will be available globally by 2027.
articles.raw_summary: CATL launches TENER sodium energy storage system, China deliveries start September, global in 2027, highlighting sodiums cost and supply advantages as it challenges lithium batteries.

articles.title: US auto regulator opens special probe after Tesla crash into Texas home killing woman inside
articles.summary: A 76-year-old woman was killed in a car crash involving a Tesla in Texas. The incident may lead to a review of safety regulations for autonomous vehicles. The US National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) has opened a special probe into the crash.
articles.raw_summary: Top US auto safety regulator NHTSA opens special probe into a Tesla Model 3 crash in Katy, Texas that killed a 76-year-old woman, examining use of automated driving as Elon Musk pushes robotaxis.

articles.title: Qualcomm nearing deal for AI chip startup Modular: Report
articles.summary: Qualcomm, a global supplier of smartphone chips, is reportedly nearing a deal with AI chip startup Modular. This move is part of Qualcomm's strategy to diversify its business beyond the volatile handset market. The deal may have implications for the tech industry, but it does not have any direct policy implications for India.
articles.raw_summary: A global supplier of smartphone chips, Qualcomm has been working to reduce its reliance on the volatile handset market by branching out into fast-growing areas like data center processors and autonomous vehicle chips.

articles.title: Oracle cut 21,000 jobs in 12 months, says AI replaced some roles
articles.summary: Oracle has cut 21,000 jobs in 12 months due to AI adoption. This is a global trend, but does not have direct implications for India's policy landscape.
articles.raw_summary: Oracle cuts 21,000 jobs in 12 months as AI adoption reshapes operations, trims global headcount and drives restructuring costs amid pressure from AI data centre spending.

articles.title: From CRED to WhatsApp: Kunal Shah takes charge of World's biggest messaging app, marking a milestone for India
articles.summary: Kunal Shah's appointment as WhatsApp's new global chief marks a rare leap from building a homegrown Indian startup to leading one of the world's most widely used consumer technology platforms. The move, announced alongside Meta's $900 million investment in CRED, underscores the growing influence of India's startup ecosystem on the global technology stage.
articles.raw_summary: Kunal Shah's appointment as WhatsApp's new global chief marks a rare leap from building a homegrown Indian startup to leading one of the world's most widely used consumer technology platforms. The move, announced alongside Meta's $900 million investment in CRED, underscores the growing influence of India's startup ecosystem on the global technology stage.

articles.title: US tech megacaps slide as SpaceX extends slump, AI expense concerns grow
articles.summary: US tech companies like Alphabet and Amazon are experiencing a slump due to concerns about AI expenses. Meanwhile, Micron and other chip stocks are surging due to high demand for AI infrastructure. This development may have implications for the global tech industry, but it does not directly affect Indian policy.
articles.raw_summary: SpaceX leads a tech megacap selloff as AI spending fears hit Alphabet and Amazon, while Micron and other chip stocks surge on AI infrastructure demand.

articles.title: CRED founder Kunal Shah to lead WhatsApp as Will Cathcart steps down
articles.summary: Meta has named CRED founder Kunal Shah as the new head of WhatsApp, succeeding Will Cathcart, who led the messaging platform for nearly seven years and helped grow it to more than 3 billion users. The leadership change comes as Meta also leads a $900 million funding round in CRED, valuing the fintech company at about $4.5 billion.
articles.raw_summary: Meta has named CRED founder Kunal Shah as the new head of WhatsApp, succeeding Will Cathcart, who led the messaging platform for nearly seven years and helped grow it to more than 3 billion users. The leadership change comes as Meta also leads a $900 million funding round in CRED, valuing the fintech company at about $4.5 billion.

articles.title: Kunal Shah is the new WhatsApp boss; Meta to invest $900 mn in CRED
articles.summary: CRED founder Kunal Shah has stepped down as CEO to join Meta as global WhatsApp chief. Meta also led a $900 million funding round in the fintech startup. The transition comes as Meta invests $900 million in CRED at a $4.5 billion valuation.

articles.title: EngageX 2026: Spotlighting customer experience in the era of AI
articles.summary: Industry leaders discussed the future of customer engagement in the era of AI at the EngageX 2026 event. They highlighted the importance of trust-led relationships and predictive intelligence. The event focused on the impact of AI on customer experience, but did not cover any policy-related topics.
articles.raw_summary: From predictive intelligence and autonomous agents to trust-led relationships, industry leaders discuss the future of customer engagement.

articles.title: China's Starlink rival launches new fundraising round, state media says
articles.summary: China's SpaceSail is launching a new fundraising round to fund its satellite project, aiming to deploy 15,000 satellites by 2030. This move is seen as a rival to Elon Musk's Starlink. The project's focus is on satellite technology, which is a key area of interest for China's space program.
articles.raw_summary: China’s SpaceSail launches new fundraising, selling up to 20% to at most three new investors, to fund LEO satellites, aiming 15,000 by 2030 and competing with Starlink.

articles.title: Galaxy S26 One UI 9 Beta 4 rollout likely around June 30: Report
articles.summary: Samsung is expected to roll out a new software update, One UI 9 Beta 4, around June 30. This update is part of the company's beta testing program. The rollout is likely to happen as per the company's previous beta update schedule.
articles.raw_summary: Beta 1 was released on May 13, followed by Beta 2 on May 26 and Beta 3 on June 16. Based on this timeline, Beta 4 is likely to be rolled out around June 30 for users enrolled in the beta program.

articles.title: Three ways Trump could get a stake in AI firms for the US
articles.summary: US President Trump is exploring ways for Americans to share profits from AI companies. This could involve government equity stakes, stock-based taxes, or funding deals similar to Intel's. The goal is to make AI more accessible and beneficial to the US population.
articles.raw_summary: Trump weighs ways for Americans to share AI profits, including government equity stakes, stock based taxes, Intel style funding deals, and citizen dividends from AI revenues

articles.title: Nadella pushes back on AI's winner-takes-all future
articles.summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has expressed concerns about the future of AI, suggesting that it may not lead to a 'winner-takes-all' scenario. He also questioned the idea that AI's primary value lies in reducing headcount, instead proposing that jobs could be reorganized.
articles.raw_summary: The Microsoft chief also challenged the growing narrative that AI's primary value lies in reducing headcount. "How about we think about reorganizing the jobs?” he said.

articles.title: Trump tells Axios he no longer views Anthropic as national security threat
articles.summary: US President Trump no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat. This is in the context of a dispute over foreign access to Anthropic's advanced AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
articles.raw_summary: Senior Anthropic technical staff were scheduled to meet with Trump administration officials earlier this week to discuss a dispute over foreign access to its most advanced AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5.

articles.title: Anthropic hires Google DeepMind's AlphaFold co-creator John Jumper
articles.summary: John Jumper, a senior research scientist at Google DeepMind, is leaving the company to join AI startup Anthropic. This move comes after Noam Shazeer announced his departure for OpenAI earlier this week. The article does not mention any specific policy implications for India, but it highlights the ongoing talent movement in the AI industry.
articles.raw_summary: Senior research scientist John Jumper said on Friday he would leave Google DeepMind to join AI startup Anthropic, the latest high-profile departure at the Big Tech giant's AI lab after Noam Shazeer announced his departure for OpenAI earlier this week.

Reliance Industries remains the most valuable firm, while Bajaj Finance leads in percentage value creation.

articles.title: Oil math: OPEC’s global crude share could slip from 35% to 31% without UAE
articles.summary: The United Arab Emirates' exit from OPEC has significantly impacted the group's global crude oil output, reducing its share from 35% to 31%. This move, effective May 1, 2026, highlights the UAE's substantial role in the bloc. Meanwhile, regional disruptions due to the Strait of Hormuz closure are escalating, with Iraq, Saudi Arabia, and Kuwait facing major production shut-ins.

articles.title: Airfares could rise by up to 25% as jet fuel costs surge, says McKinsey
articles.summary: Global jet fuel supplies are facing significant pressure due to geopolitical events and refinery limitations, according to McKinsey. This is driving up costs for airlines, with potential airfare hikes of 20-25% expected as fuel prices surge. Inventories remain low, and key exporting regions are restricting shipments, creating a challenging outlook for the summer travel season.

articles.title: Crude prices drop to lowest since February on Iran supply hopes
articles.summary: Global crude oil prices have plunged to four-month lows, driven by improved tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and anticipation of increased Iranian oil exports. This easing of supply disruption fears has led to significant drops in Brent and WTI futures. Despite this, underlying geopolitical uncertainties persist, with investors closely monitoring US-Iran negotiations and inventory data for future market direction.

articles.title: Indonesian proverb of the day: 'An elephant at the edge of the eye is not seen, but an ant across the ocean is seen'
articles.summary: This ancient Indonesian proverb, 'An elephant at the edge of one’s own eye goes unnoticed, while an ant across the ocean is clearly seen,' captures the essence of our inclination to criticize minor issues in others while overlooking our own significant shortcomings. With deep roots in oral tradition, it aligns with contemporary psychology regarding cognitive bias.

articles.title: Mongolian proverb of the day: 'Suffer with your own rule, rather than frolic under someone else’s rule'
articles.summary: In Mongolia, a saying goes, 'Suffer with your own rule, rather than frolic under someone else’s rule.' This wisdom reflects a strong cultural belief in self-sufficiency over ease. Born from the expansive steppes and their nomadic lifestyle, the phrase underscores the importance of personal freedom and decision-making, even if those choices come with challenges. It’s a timeless reminder that true integrity stems from self-determination.

articles.title: Explained: What is desk-side computing and why Cisco thinks it could be AI’s next big shift
articles.summary: Cisco believes that AI workloads may shift from distant data centers to machines located next to users, which could reshape personal computing and enterprise networks. This shift could have significant implications for the tech industry, but it does not have any direct policy implications for India at this time.
articles.raw_summary: As AI agents become more autonomous and computationally intensive, some workloads may shift from distant data centres to dedicated machines located next to users. Cisco believes this hybrid approach could reshape both personal computing and enterprise networks.

articles.title: Explained: What is Section 301 and why is India concerned about it?
articles.summary: As India and the US work towards an interim trade pact, experts say supply-chain traceability and compliance standards are becoming as important as tariffs themselves, while warning that unilateral trade actions could undermine the predictability businesses seek.

articles.title: Opinion | Meta's bet goes beyond CRED
articles.summary: Meta’s investment in CRED and the quasi Acqui-Hire of Kunal Shah are less a verdict on the company than a recognition of entrepreneurial capability and India’s centrality to WhatsApp’s future. The move reflects Meta’s long-standing preference for acquiring builders and signals another strategic wager on India as a market, a laboratory, and a source of leadership.

articles.title: Mint Explainer| What would it take for India to create a SpaceX?
articles.summary: SpaceX’s $2.5 trillion valuation is driven by its reusable rocket technology, which slashed launch costs and dominates the global space market.
